venues.name: Riverside Cultural Center
venues.description: A mid-sized cultural hub along the riverfront featuring a black-box theatre, rehearsal rooms, and flexible gallery spaces for community arts events.
venues.accessibility_notes: Step-free entrance from Riverfront Ave, ramps to all public levels, accessible restrooms on each floor, assistive listening devices available upon request.
venues.directions_driving: From downtown Riverton, take Main St north to Riverfront Ave. Turn right and continue 0.5 miles; the center is on the left. Underground parking garage is accessible from 2nd St.
venues.directions_walking: From Central Station, walk north on Main St for 8 minutes, turn right onto Riverfront Ave, and continue two blocks. The entrance is on the left, across from the river promenade.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Served by Bus 12 (Riverfront), Bus 7 (Uptown–Riverside), and Light Rail Line B (Riverside stop two blocks away).

venues.name: Downtown Community Arts Center
venues.description: A renovated warehouse hosting exhibitions, music performances, and hands-on arts workshops for all ages.
venues.accessibility_notes: Automatic doors at main entrance, elevator access to mezzanine classrooms, accessible seating in performance hall. Please contact us ahead of time for specific accommodation requests.
venues.directions_driving: Exit Hwy 80 at 7th St, continue north to Market St, then turn right. Limited street parking; recommended to use nearby public garages on 5th or 8th St.
venues.directions_walking: From Civic Plaza, walk three blocks east along Market St. The center is on the right, with a large mural on the facade.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Located one block from Civic Plaza Station (Lines A, C, and D) and directly on Bus 3 and 5 routes.

venues.name: Greenfield Park
venues.description: A large urban park with open lawns, playgrounds, and an outdoor stage used for family concerts and festivals.
venues.accessibility_notes: Paved paths connect parking areas to the playground and stage. Some lawn seating areas have uneven ground; a paved viewing area is reserved for wheelchair users.
venues.directions_driving: From Hwy 101, take the Greenfield Blvd exit and head west for 1 mile. Park entrances are on both sides of the boulevard with two main parking lots.
venues.directions_walking: From Greenfield Station, follow the signed pedestrian path north for 6 minutes to the south park entrance.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Served by Bus 2 and 9 with stops along Greenfield Blvd; Greenfield light rail station is a 6-minute walk.

venues.name: Harborview Senior Center
venues.description: A welcoming community center offering programs, social events, and wellness activities tailored for older adults.
venues.accessibility_notes: Fully accessible single-level facility with grab bars in restrooms, wide corridors, and curb cuts at the main entrance. Staff can assist with mobility devices.
venues.directions_driving: Take the Harborview exit off Bayside Fwy and follow signs toward the marina. Turn left on Harborview Lane; the center is at the end of the cul-de-sac with onsite parking.
venues.directions_walking: From Harborview Ferry Terminal, walk west along the waterfront path for 5 minutes until you see the center on your right.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Accessible via Bus 21 (Harborview Loop) and community shuttle S1 from Central Station.

venues.name: Elm Street Neighborhood Library
venues.description: A cozy branch library hosting storytelling, kids’ workshops, and quiet cultural programs.
venues.accessibility_notes: Ramp access at side entrance, elevator to second-floor meeting room, and low-height service desks.
venues.directions_driving: From Downtown, take Elm St west for 1.2 miles. Limited street parking is available on Elm and 3rd Ave.
venues.directions_walking: From Elmwood Plaza, walk north on 3rd Ave, then turn right on Elm St. The library is on the left in the middle of the block.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Bus 6 and 14 stop at Elm & 3rd; Bus 14 is wheelchair-lift equipped on all scheduled runs.

venues.name: Westside Youth Center
venues.description: A dedicated space for children and teens featuring art studios, a small stage, and multi-purpose rooms.
venues.accessibility_notes: Ground-floor access to all activity rooms, with one accessible restroom near the main lobby. Stage has a ramp on the left side.
venues.directions_driving: From Greenfield Blvd, head south on Sunset Ave and turn right onto Sunset Terrace. The youth center is on the corner, with parking shared with the adjacent school after hours.
venues.directions_walking: From Westside Station, walk east two blocks along Sunset Terrace. The entrance is marked by bright banners.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Served by Bus 5 and community school shuttles; nearest stop is Sunset Terrace & 2nd.

venues.name: Old Mill Theater
venues.description: A historic theatre converted from an old textile mill, now hosting plays, dance performances, and film screenings.
venues.accessibility_notes: Accessible entrance via side ramp on Mill Lane, with designated wheelchair seating and companion seats in the main auditorium.
venues.directions_driving: Exit Bayside Fwy at Mill Rd, follow signs for Old Mill District, then turn onto Mill Lane. Parking is available in the adjacent lot after 5 PM.
venues.directions_walking: From Old Mill Plaza, walk one block south on Mill Rd, then left onto Mill Lane. The theatre marquee is visible from the plaza.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Served by Bus 8 and the Old Mill streetcar; closest stop is Mill Rd & 1st.

venues.name: East River Outdoor Stage
venues.description: An open-air performance stage along the east riverbank, used for seasonal festivals, concerts, and film nights.
venues.accessibility_notes: Accessible viewing platforms near front-of-house, paved paths from the main entrance, and accessible portable restrooms during events.
venues.directions_driving: From downtown, follow signs to East Riverfront and use the Riverwalk East parking lot. The stage is a short signed walk from the lot.
venues.directions_walking: From Riverside Cultural Center, follow the riverwalk path east for about 10 minutes; the stage is on your left.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Access via Bus 12 (get off at Riverwalk East) or Light Rail Line B to Riverside, then a 10-minute walk.

venues.name: Market Square Plaza
venues.description: A central urban plaza used for the annual Community Street Festival, pop-up markets, and outdoor performances.
venues.accessibility_notes: Flat, paved surfaces throughout the plaza, with curb cuts on all corners and accessible portable restrooms during large events.
venues.directions_driving: From any downtown exit, follow signs to Civic Center. Underground municipal garages are located beneath the plaza and on adjacent streets.
venues.directions_walking: Directly across from City Hall; most downtown hotels are within a 10–15 minute walk.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Civic Plaza Station (heavy rail and streetcar) opens directly onto the plaza; multiple bus routes stop along Market St.

venues.name: North Hill Recreation Center
venues.description: A multi-purpose recreation facility with a gymnasium, classrooms, and a small auditorium for community events.
venues.accessibility_notes: Accessible entrance next to the main parking lot, elevator access to second-floor auditorium, and visual emergency alarms throughout.
venues.directions_driving: Take North Hill Rd exit and follow the road uphill for 0.8 miles. The center’s parking lot is on the right just past Oak St.
venues.directions_walking: From North Hill Park, walk south along North Hill Rd for 5 minutes. The building is on the left.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Served by Bus 18 (North Hill Loop), with a stop directly in front of the main entrance.

venues.name: Southside Cultural Hall
venues.description: A neighborhood hall used for dance performances, music recitals, and cultural celebrations.
venues.accessibility_notes: Main hall is up a short flight of stairs and currently does not have a lift. Not recommended for attendees using wheelchairs.
venues.directions_driving: From Bayside Fwy, take Maple Ave exit and continue south for 0.5 miles. Street parking is available on Maple and 10th St.
venues.directions_walking: From Southside Park, walk east along Maple Ave for three blocks. The hall is on the right.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Bus 4 stops at Maple & 9th, one block from the hall.

venues.name: Lakeview Botanical Garden
venues.description: Scenic gardens around a small lake, hosting outdoor art walks, nature tours, and seasonal festivals.
venues.accessibility_notes: Main loop path around the lake is paved and mostly level, though some side paths have steeper grades. Accessible parking is available near the main gate.
venues.directions_driving: From downtown, take Lakeview Dr north for 2 miles. The garden entrance and parking lot are on the left, opposite Lakeview High School.
venues.directions_walking: From Lakeview High, cross Lakeview Dr at the signal and follow the sidewalk to the garden gate.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Bus 10 terminates at Lakeview Garden stop at the front gate.

venues.name: Arts District Makers Space
venues.description: A collaborative makerspace with printmaking studios, ceramics facilities, and flexible classrooms for arts & crafts workshops.
venues.accessibility_notes: Ground-floor studios with wide doors and adjustable-height work tables. Some equipment requires standing; staff can help adapt setups as needed.
venues.directions_driving: From Market St, turn south on Studio Row and continue three blocks. Limited visitor parking is available behind Building B.
venues.directions_walking: From Downtown Community Arts Center, walk east along Market St, then south on Studio Row. Look for the red brick building marked Building B.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Within a 5-minute walk of Studio Row Station (Streetcar Line E) and Bus 3 and 5 stops on Market St.

venues.name: Heritage House Museum
venues.description: A historic home museum offering cultural history exhibits, small lectures, and storytelling evenings.
venues.accessibility_notes: Historic building with narrow doorways and stairs to all exhibit floors; not fully accessible for wheelchair users. A virtual tour is available online.
venues.directions_driving: Take Main St east to Heritage Way and turn left. A small visitor lot is behind the building; additional parking is on nearby side streets.
venues.directions_walking: From Riverside Cultural Center, walk south along Main St, then left on Heritage Way. The house is on the corner with a wrought-iron fence.
venues.directions_public_transit_summary: Bus 1 stops at Main & Heritage; the museum is a 2-minute walk from the stop.

events.title: Family Music Night at Riverside
events.description: An early-evening concert featuring local youth bands and sing-along favorites, with activity tables set up in the lobby for kids.

events.title: Saturday Morning Garden Art Walk
events.description: A relaxed, family-friendly walk through the botanical garden with sketching prompts and simple nature-inspired art activities.

events.title: North Hill Sunday Puppet Matinee
events.description: Colorful hand-puppet stories based on local folktales, followed by a short meet-the-puppets session for kids.

events.title: Seniors Wellness & Music Hour
events.description: A short talk on healthy aging followed by a relaxed live music set tailored for older adults, with time for questions and conversation.

events.title: Friday Afternoon Chamber Concert
events.description: An intimate hour of classical chamber works performed by the Riverside String Quartet in the black-box theatre.

events.title: Community Street Festival – Market Square
events.description: The annual Community Street Festival with live music, food vendors, information booths, kids’ activities, and neighborhood organizations.

events.title: Jazz on the River
events.description: An outdoor evening of jazz standards and original compositions performed on the East River Outdoor Stage.

events.title: Spring Dance Showcase
events.description: A mixed bill of contemporary, folkloric, and ballroom dance pieces performed by community ensembles.

events.title: New Voices Theatre Night
events.description: An evening of short plays by emerging playwrights, staged by the Old Mill community theatre troupe.

events.title: Family Film in the Park
events.description: Bring a blanket for an outdoor screening of a beloved animated classic on the Greenfield Park lawn.

events.title: Community Storytelling Circle
events.description: Neighbors of all ages share personal stories, folktales, and poems in an intimate circle at the Elm Street Library.

events.title: Seniors Spring Social Dance
events.description: An afternoon of social dancing with a live band, light refreshments, and dance hosts to help everyone feel welcome.

events.title: Makers Market & Open Studios
events.description: Browse handmade goods from local artists, watch live demos, and tour the Arts District Makers Space.

events.title: Youth Poetry Slam Night
events.description: Teens share original spoken word pieces in a supportive slam-style event with guest judges and open mic slots.

transit_routes.description: Runs every 15 minutes between Central Station and Riverside Cultural Center along Riverfront Ave.

stops.stop_description: Main downtown hub with connections to all rail lines.
Transfer point for multiple bus routes near Market Square Plaza.
Closest stop to Riverside Cultural Center, 2-minute level walk.
Access to East River Outdoor Stage and riverwalk path.

transit_routes.description: Light rail service connecting Riverside to uptown and the university district.

stops.stop_description: Northern terminus with park-and-ride facilities.
Underground station beneath Market Square Plaza.
Two blocks from Riverside Cultural Center via signed pedestrian route.
Access stop for East River Outdoor Stage and riverfront trail.

transit_routes.description: Local bus serving Greenfield neighborhood and connecting to downtown.

stops.stop_description: Connection to regional rail and other bus lines.
South entrance to Greenfield Park and playground.
North entrance to Greenfield Park near the outdoor stage.

transit_routes.description: Crosstown service linking Westside, Greenfield, and Lakeview.

stops.stop_description: Transfers to school shuttles and neighborhood routes.
Mid-block stop directly across from the main park entrance.
Connection point for Bus 10 to Lakeview Botanical Garden.

transit_routes.description: Loop route circulating between Central Station, Harborview Senior Center, and the ferry terminal.

stops.stop_description: Start of the Harborview Loop with elevator access to all platforms.
Stop directly in front of Harborview Senior Center with curb cuts.
Connection to regional ferry services and waterfront promenade.

transit_routes.description: Community shuttle with priority seating, running weekdays between Central Station and Harborview.

stops.stop_description: Signed shuttle pick-up area on the east side of the station.
Mid-route stop for waterfront parks and paths.
Drop-off at the main entrance with covered waiting area.

transit_routes.description: Heavy rail line stopping directly beneath Market Square Plaza at Civic Plaza Station.

stops.stop_description: Park-and-ride lot serving North Hill Recreation Center.
Direct station access to Market Square Plaza and City Hall.
Transfer point to ferry services and waterfront buses.

transit_routes.description: Historic streetcar running the length of Market Street with a stop at Market Square.

stops.stop_description: Access stop for Old Mill Theater and nearby galleries.
Street-level stop at the south edge of Market Square Plaza.
Short walk to Downtown Community Arts Center and Studio Row.

transit_routes.description: Neighborhood route terminating at the Lakeview Botanical Garden front gate.

stops.stop_description: Connection to other downtown routes and Civic Plaza Station.
Crosswalk access to the high school and residential area.
End-of-line stop at the main entrance to the gardens.

transit_routes.description: Loop route connecting North Hill Recreation Center with North Hill Park and Civic Plaza.

stops.stop_description: Downtown connection point to rail and multiple buses.
Stop at the main entrance to North Hill Park.
Curb-side stop at the recreation center’s main parking lot.

workshops.title: Printmaking Intensive – Full Day
workshops.description: Explore relief and monoprint techniques in this hands-on, full-day arts & crafts intensive. All materials provided.

workshops.title: Weekend Watercolor for Beginners
workshops.description: Learn basic watercolor washes, layering, and simple landscapes in a relaxed weekend workshop.

workshops.title: Evening Ceramics Hand-Building
workshops.description: Create your own mugs and bowls using simple hand-building techniques. Pieces will be fired and ready for pickup in two weeks.

workshops.title: Kids Recycled Art Lab
workshops.description: Kids transform everyday recyclables into imaginative sculptures while learning about reuse and the environment.

workshops.title: Saturday Cartooning Club
workshops.description: Young artists learn to create their own comic characters and short strips using pencils and markers.

workshops.title: Young Makers: Intro to 3D Printing
workshops.description: Kids design simple objects on tablets and watch them come to life on the makerspace’s 3D printers.

workshops.title: Family Music Jam
workshops.description: Parents and kids explore rhythm and song together using drums, shakers, and simple call-and-response games.

workshops.title: Seniors Gentle Movement & Art
workshops.description: A low-impact movement session followed by simple creative exercises to support balance, memory, and social connection.

workshops.title: Teen Digital Storytelling Lab
workshops.description: Teens combine photos, audio, and video clips to create short digital stories about their lives and communities.

workshops.title: Community Choir Workshop
workshops.description: Sing in harmony with neighbors while learning vocal warm-ups and accessible choral pieces. No experience required.

workshops.title: Evening Theatre Improv Lab
workshops.description: Play theatre games, build characters, and practice quick-thinking skills in this supportive improv lab for adults.

workshops.title: Mosaic Garden Stepping Stones
workshops.description: Design and create colorful mosaic stepping stones to brighten your garden path. Suitable for adults and older kids with an adult.

volunteer_opportunities.name: Community Street Festival Volunteer Crew
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help run the annual Community Street Festival at Market Square Plaza, including info booths, kid zones, stage assistance, and setup/teardown.

volunteer_opportunities.name: Riverside Cultural Center Event Support
volunteer_opportunities.description: Provide front-of-house support for concerts, theatre events, and family programs at Riverside Cultural Center (greeting guests, tickets, seating).

volunteer_opportunities.name: Family Music Night Ushers & Activity Helpers
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist families attending Family Music Night by handing out programs, guiding them to seats, and supporting lobby activity tables.

volunteer_opportunities.name: Makers Market & Open Studios Volunteers
volunteer_opportunities.description: Support the Makers Market by helping with artist check-in, wayfinding, and kids craft corners at the Arts District Makers Space.

volunteer_opportunities.name: Lakeview Garden Events Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist with outdoor cultural events at Lakeview Botanical Garden, including guiding visitors, staffing info tables, and light setup.

volunteer_shifts.title: Saturday Morning Setup Crew
volunteer_shifts.notes: Lift and carry up to 25 lbs; closed-toe shoes required.

volunteer_shifts.title: Saturday Morning Info Booth & Greeters
volunteer_shifts.notes: Ideal for people who enjoy answering questions and welcoming visitors.

volunteer_shifts.title: Saturday Kids Zone Assistants
volunteer_shifts.notes: This shift is now full. Supports arts & crafts and games in the kids area.

volunteer_shifts.title: Afternoon Stage & Performer Support
volunteer_shifts.notes: Some roles involve standing for longer periods near the main stage.

volunteer_shifts.title: Afternoon Info Booth & Floaters
volunteer_shifts.notes: Assist with wayfinding and quick attendee surveys.

volunteer_shifts.title: Evening Teardown & Cleanup Crew
volunteer_shifts.notes: May involve heavier lifting; gloves provided.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Morning Post-Event Cleanup
volunteer_shifts.notes: Light cleanup and sorting of lost & found items.

volunteer_shifts.title: Family Music Night Ushers
volunteer_shifts.notes: Includes a brief orientation at the start of the shift.

volunteer_shifts.title: Family Music Night Activity Table Helpers
volunteer_shifts.notes: Helps kids with simple music-themed crafts in the lobby.

volunteer_shifts.title: Chamber Concert Front-of-House
volunteer_shifts.notes: Assist with will-call tickets and seating guests.

volunteer_shifts.title: Weekend Evening Event Support
volunteer_shifts.notes: Supports a rotating series of Friday night performances.

volunteer_shifts.title: Community Choir Workshop Support
volunteer_shifts.notes: Helps with registration and light room reset after the workshop.

volunteer_shifts.title: Makers Market Morning Setup & Artist Check-In
volunteer_shifts.notes: Helps artists unload and find their booths; some lifting involved.

volunteer_shifts.title: Makers Market Floor Support
volunteer_shifts.notes: Great for people who like to walk and chat with visitors.

volunteer_shifts.title: Makers Market Kids Craft Corner
volunteer_shifts.notes: Supports simple drop-in art projects; all instructions provided.

volunteer_shifts.title: Garden Art Walk Guides
volunteer_shifts.notes: Leads small groups along accessible garden paths; script provided.

volunteer_shifts.title: Mosaic Workshop Setup & Support
volunteer_shifts.notes: Helps set out materials and assist participants during the workshop.

volunteer_shifts.title: Spring Garden Concert Evening Support
volunteer_shifts.notes: Outdoor evening concert; dress in layers and comfortable shoes.
